records.ety: [OE. wrestlen, wrastlen, AS. wr<?/stlian, freq. of wr<?/stan to wrest; akin to OD. wrastelen to wrestle. See Wrest, v. t.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Wrestled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Wrestling (?).]

defs: To contend, by grappling with, and striving to trip or throw down, an opponent; as, they wrestled skillfully.
quotes: To-morrow, sir, I wrestle for my credit, and he that escapes me without some broken limb shall acquit him well.
Another, by a fall in wrestling, started the end of the clavicle from the sternum.

defs: Hence, to struggle; to strive earnestly; to contend.
quotes: We wrestle not against flesh and blood.
Difficulties with which he had himself wrestled.

defs: To wrestle with; to seek to throw down as in wrestling.
A struggle between two persons to see which will throw the other down; a bout at wrestling; a wrestling match; a struggle.
quotes: Whom in a wrestle the giant catching aloft, with a terrible hug broke three of his ribs.
